---
title: "{{< meta course_title >}}"
deck_id: d05
deck_title: "{{< meta decks.d05.title >}}"
deck_short_title: "{{< meta decks.d05.short_title >}}"

include-after-body:
  - text: |
      <script id="deck-nav-meta" type="application/json">
      {
        "deck_prev_file": "{{< meta decks.d04.file >}}",
        "deck_prev_title": "{{< meta decks.d04.title >}}",
        "deck_next_file": "",
        "deck_next_title": ""
      }
      </script>

subtitle: |
  <span class="deck-title">{{< meta decks.d05.title >}}</span><br>
  <span class="deck-meta">{{< meta texts.wms.short >}} Ch. 15<br>
  Project Presentations on April 20–22<br>
  Monday, May 4, 8:00 AM, in PH 108 <br>
  <span class="indent">Comprehensive Final Exam </span>
  </span>
---

# Why nonparametric methods?

- So far:
  - Paired t-test
  - Two-sample t-test
  - One-way ANOVA

- These rely on:
  - Normality (or large $n$)
  - Mean as a meaningful summary

- What if:
  - Data are skewed?
  - Outliers are present?
  - Only ordering is trustworthy?

---

# Key idea

- Replace raw data with:
  - signs
  - ranks

- Benefits:
  - fewer assumptions
  - robustness

- Tradeoff:
  - may lose efficiency if parametric assumptions hold

---

# Roadmap

| Setting | Parametric | Nonparametric |
|---|---|---|
| Matched pairs | Paired t-test | Sign, Wilcoxon signed-rank |
| Two samples | Two-sample t-test | Mann–Whitney |
| $m$ groups | One-way ANOVA | Kruskal–Wallis |

---

# Matched-pairs setup

- Observations come in pairs:
  $$
  (X_1,Y_1), \dots, (X_n,Y_n)
  $$

- Define differences:
  $$
  D_i = X_i - Y_i
  $$

- Parametric approach:
  - Paired t-test uses $\bar{D}$

- Nonparametric:
  - Use signs or ranks of $D_i$

---

# Sign test

- Use only:
  $$
  \text{sign}(D_i)
  $$

- Ignore zeros

- Let:
  $$
  S = \#\{D_i > 0\}
  $$

---

# Sign test: hypothesis

- Null:
  $$
  P(D_i > 0) = \tfrac{1}{2}
  $$

- Under $H_0$:
  $$
  S \sim \text{Binomial}(n, 1/2)
  $$

---

# Wilcoxon signed-rank test

- Steps:
  1. Compute $D_i$
  2. Remove zeros
  3. Rank $|D_i|$
  4. Attach sign back

---

# Two independent samples

- Data:
  $$
  X_1,\dots,X_{n_1}, \quad Y_1,\dots,Y_{n_2}
  $$

- Nonparametric:
  - compare relative positions

---

# Mann–Whitney test

- Pool all observations
- Rank them
- Compare rank sums

---

# More than two groups

- Data:
  $$
  m \text{ groups}
  $$

- Nonparametric:
  - Kruskal–Wallis

---

# Summary

- Use signs and ranks
- Fewer assumptions
- More robust
